reviews.review_description:
The studio that brought us the fantastic Retropolis series of games is back with a new game that marries point & click gameplay in an occult setting.

There's a surreal blend of comedy and weirdness to carry you through the numerous puzzles and story

To anyone that is a fan of the traditional point-and-click puzzle games, it's a no-brainer.

It's also highly recommended to anyone that loves their puzzle games in general and not just the Retropolis series (which you should also get!).

The game launched in early access form at a super low price and is representative of the full game.

Support the team as they continue to update the title for free.

Anyone that's familiar with EA titles on PC will know the drill, but with Riddlewood Manor it's all but guaranteed we will receive the full game (the full flat game has already had its full release on Steam).

I can't wait to jump into the updates as and when they come. 

But for anyone else, pick it up at the cheapest price before the inevitable price increase when it reaches its full release.

developer_response.body: This made our day :)Thank you for such a thoughtful write-up. You've captured the spirit of Riddlewood Manor really well: the weirdness, the laughs, and the puzzles are all very much intentional. We're glad the early access approach feels transparent and fair, and we're excited to keep delivering updates as we build toward the full release. If you loved the Retropolis series too, you're exactly the kind of player we make games for. Come hang out with us in the community. It's a great place to follow along as things develop.

reviews.review_description:
Riddlewood manor review.
I won the key for this game and was excited to give it a try.
At first I was a bit clueless, just clicking on things, collecting them and moving on, after ringing the doorbell a couple of times and getting killed by the demon, I started to look and think and change my way of playing.
Once you get in the groove of things, the puzzles make sense and the simple but effective graphics are great.
The puzzles require some thought but are never too difficult that you find yourself stuck and wanting to give up.
I did have one glitch, when I started  the second part, entering the house,  my inventory disappeared, and no amount of button pressing could get it back, but a game restart sorted it and everything has been fine since.
I've not finished it yet, but I am far enough in to say this is an enjoyable puzzler with enough to keep you wanting to progress, and some mild jump scares to keep with the feel of the game.
If you like a logical thinking puzzle game with a dark theme, then for the price point you can't go wrong.
I would recommend it to all, especially if you don't have time for a very long play session, it auto saves after each part so it's great for a quick play.

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving Riddlewood Manor a go, and for sticking with it past that first encounter with the demon! We love hearing that the puzzles clicked once you found your rhythm. The inventory disappearing at the start of Part 2 is a bug we're aware of, and we're actively working on a fix; in the meantime, a restart does sort it out as you found. We hope the back half of the manor treats you well, and we'd love to hear what you think once you've seen it through to the end.

reviews.review_description:
An exceptional puzzle game, with a splash of cartoonish mild horror, and jump scares. Not too difficult to get stumped and give up, but not so easy that you lose interest in playing. The graphics and artwork look great. The audio and voiceovers are decent. It’s basically point and click, so no complaints with the controls.
You go through the different levels collecting items into your inventory, then using those items as puzzle pieces to progress through the levels. Everything you find and pick up in the levels combines, and interacts with something else in the levels to complete the level.
Everything runs smooth and has no problems, except one thing. A couple of times my inventory box disappeared after entering a door. If this happens you have no access to your collected items, and can’t progress through the level. Reloading your save point fixes this issue, and you can continue playing normally. I’m sure the developers will release an update in the near future that fixes this annoying, but not game-breaking bug.
If you like puzzle games this is a good one, and I would definitely recommend Riddlewood Manor to anyone that does enjoy puzzle games. Congratulations to the developers for creating a fantastic game!👍🏻👍🏻
It’s worth noting that I won this game in a giveaway on Facebook in the Meta Quest Promotions, Giveaways, and Referrals group. Thanks to the admins of that group for getting the word out about this awesome game.

Early access review - loved it.  Interesting how point and click can be puzzling.  Love what the team has done so far,  can't wait for more. 

Only feedback is that when I died and continued, the inventory tab was not visible.  Had to restart the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! This kind of feedback genuinely means a lot to us during early access. We're glad the puzzles are landing the way we hoped. On the inventory tab disappearing after a continue: that's a real bug and we're actively looking into it. In the meantime, if it helps to share any additional details (platform, save type), feel free to reach out to us directly. More content is on the way, and we hope you'll stick around for it.

reviews.review_title: In early access, but I love what’s here
reviews.review_description:
This is a puzzle game ported from a flat screen game so there is not a lot of VR interactions. It’s point and click just like the game description says, and I am totally fine with that. The art style is great, the puzzles so far have been just challenging enough to be fun but not frustrating. 
  The devs in discord have already mentioned fixing the one bug I encountered, and are working on adding more rooms/ puzzles to the game is it progresses to full release. 
  I am excited to play more of this!

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to share this. It genuinely means a lot to hear the puzzle difficulty is landing in that sweet spot. You're right that this is a point-and-click experience rather than a full VR interaction set, and we appreciate you going in with clear expectations and still finding it worthwhile. The bug you hit is already on our radar and being addressed, and adding more rooms and puzzles as we move toward full release is very much the plan. Can't wait for you to see what's coming.

reviews.review_description:
I played the early access version of Riddlewood Manor, a point-and-click horror/adventure puzzle game (seated).

The puzzles are thoughtfully designed and often multi-layered—you’ll frequently need to solve one puzzle to uncover information for another. It rewards careful observation and a bit of patience.

There are jump scares, but they’re more “cartoon startling” than true horror—think surprise rather than dread. You can die and restart, but there are multiple save points, which makes it forgiving and keeps you from having to redo completed puzzles.

The visuals are hand-drawn in a stylized cartoon format and are quite nicely done, adding to the atmosphere without being overly dark or heavy.

I did run into a few elements that seemed like puzzles (such as wall plates that appear rearrangeable), but I wasn’t able to solve them. I’m hoping those are placeholders for future content rather than something I missed.

The only technical issue I encountered was the inventory occasionally disappearing. Reloading the game brought it back, and I didn’t lose any noticeable progress.

At this point, I’ve reached several doors marked “coming soon,” so it seems I’ve hit the current end of available content. Overall, I enjoyed the experience and am looking forward to future updates. Thanks to the developers.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for this thoughtful write-up. You've captured the spirit of what we're going for really well, and it's great to hear the multi-layered puzzle design and hand-drawn visuals are landing. The inventory disappearing is a known bug we're actively working on, and those wall plates you noticed are indeed placeholders for content still in development. Stay with us as we keep building out the manor.

reviews.review_description:
I'd probably give this 5 stars, however, every time I move to a new room or die and have to start over I lose my inventory. The entire inventory square itself completely disappears. It's so frustrating that I'd ask for my money back but they'd probably tell me no. 

The game itself is actually pretty fun and the puzzles aren't impossible or too difficult.  The graphics are nice and I like the art style. There are funny moments and of course a few jump scares. Nothing that'll give ya a heart attack or anything like that. For some reason when you die you have to start at the beginning of the game. Not necessarily having to solve the puzzles over again....just have to go through the level again. I know.......weird.  It's only extremely difficult to proceed in the game when the inventory keeps disappearing. When it gets fixed I'd be happy to adjust my rating because it's definitely worth playing with great potential. Thank you for reading & have an awesome day!

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write such a detailed and genuinely kind review. It means a lot to hear that the puzzles, art style, and humor are landing the way we hoped. The inventory disappearing on room transitions and deaths is a real bug and not the experience we want anyone to have, so we're actively looking into it. The checkpoint behavior you mentioned (replaying levels without re-solving puzzles) is also on our radar. We'd love to earn that 5-star rating once things are working as they should.

reviews.review_title: This game is a must buy!
reviews.review_description:
I’ve never tried a point-and-click in VR, and I’m glad this was my very first one. At the time of this writing, the game is currently in early access and worth every penny. You play as a… actually, I’m not sure who you play as… but you’re tasked with dispelling the evil within the Manor through solving multi-stage puzzles to progress and eradicate the Evelin presence. The game is high quality, but also doesn’t take itself too seriously. With early cccess, you’re able to play through the first portion of the game, and I think you can lock in this price when the full game releases.

Don’t sleep on this! That said, you probably wont sleep after playing this either.

developer_response.body: That closing line might be our favorite review ending yet. We're so glad Riddlewood Manor made a great first impression on the genre, and thanks for jumping in during early access. While you wait for more of the Manor, our Retropolis games are worth a visit too. They're point-and-click VR mysteries set in a retro-futuristic cartoon world, so if you liked this style, you'll feel right at home.

reviews.review_title: A Top VR Puzzle Adventure Full of Chills
reviews.review_description: Riddlewood Manor is an outstanding point-and-click adventure and puzzle game with stunning visuals! Much more challenging than Peanut Button’s previous title, Retropolis, it offers a real test for puzzle lovers. The haunted house theme is beautifully executed in cell-shaded graphics, and the well-placed jump scares add just the right amount of thrill. Immersive, clever, and genuinely fun — a must-play for anyone who loves mysteries, puzzles, and a few chills along the way!

developer_response.body: The comparison to Retropolis means a lot to us, and we're really glad the difficulty step up landed well. Stylized haunted houses and a well-timed jump scare or two. That's exactly the vibe we were going for. Thanks for playing early; there's plenty more to come as we continue building out Riddlewood Manor.

reviews.review_description:
Loved playing Retroplis Games and this one is a great follow up.  Have only done a few levels and I like the game play. Puzzles are tricky but you can figure them out with trial and error. 

One glitch for the developers that I found. During the level in dining room making breakfast if you fail and start over your inventory board disappears. I had to quit the game and restart it to get it back. 

other than that loving it

developer_response.body: Really glad to hear Riddlewood Manor is hitting the mark as a follow-up, and we love that the puzzles are landing that sweet spot of tricky-but-solvable. The inventory on restart is a known bug we're working on. Thanks for playing early and sticking with us as we continue building the game out!

reviews.review_description: Simple but effective graphics and a great mix of puzzles (from easy to near impossible to figure out). Some frustrations, such as why does the gas have to be lit before the pan will go on the hob? Biggest problem I found was the inventory completely disappearing after a death/restart. Only fixed with an exit and reopen game. Also a button is needed to view the inventory rather than have it floating in the way all the time.

developer_response.body: Really glad the puzzle variety is hitting the mark for you. The inventory disappearing after a death is a known bug we're actively working to fix, and the idea of a toggle button for the inventory instead of it floating is genuinely good feedback we'd love to explore. Thanks for playing during early access and sticking with us as we build the manor out.

reviews.review_description: I broke my only review games I have completed rule and gave this one a not so good review. It wasn't bad, I just said I didn't get it. I didn't see any clues, and it seemed more like guessing than having to find clues and figure things out. The mistake I made was thinking each room was its own complete puzzle, when different rooms give clues to other rooms. I played more. I enjoyed it. It's cute. I love cartoonish games. I'm still not finished, but i wanted to update my review. It is well worth the $5. I wasn't going to refund because it was cheap already, but I'm glad I still have it to play. It's hard for me to decide on stars, but I like to decide between the price, graphics, controls and the length of the game. I'm gonna do 5 now, I'll change it if the rest stinks, but I doubt it. Sorry for the first review developers.

developer_response.body: The frustration of feeling like you're guessing rather than solving is real, and we take that seriously. Riddlewood Manor is still in early access, and puzzle clarity is one of the areas we're actively refining based on exactly this kind of feedback. If you do give it another go, we'd genuinely love to hear which specific moments lost you so we can dig into whether the logic needs better signposting. You can reach us at support or drop a note in our community. Stay with us as we continue building the game out.

reviews.review_description: A great little game and I can’t wait to play more of it.  The only issue I had was that my inventory disappeared a couple of times when I entered another room and I had to reload the game. Otherwise this is definitely a game to have.

developer_response.body: Really glad the game is clicking for you, and thanks for flagging the inventory bug. It's a known issue and we have a fix coming in the next update. Stay with us as we keep building out the early access.

reviews.review_description:
*Early Access*

This is a kind of point and click VR puzzler, done in a comic style… almost like an animated comic style.

I like the theme. It warns that it may not be for everyone.

The sound is decent too.

Game play is crazy, little jump scares… click on things to find items that you use to solve puzzles and move on to other rooms… maybe escape room ish.

A nice style puzzle adventure… with an adult theme.

Nicely done.. look forward to the finish product.

developer_response.body: That 'animated comic style' description is exactly the vibe we were going for, so it's great to hear it's coming through. Really glad the atmosphere, sound, and puzzles are clicking for you. Stick with us as we build out the rest of the manor.

reviews.review_description:
The game as of today is an hour long. Quite fun. The only bug was inventory UI disappearing sometimes. I highly recommend this pre-order. I played the full available content and I didn't want to end, but alas, it's early access.

Get it now. Support the devs. Save some money.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for jumping in early and putting it so well. The inventory UI disappearing is a known bug we're actively working on, and there's a lot more manor to explore as we build this out. Stay with us!

reviews.review_description:
Absolutely love the art style of this game, it captures the whole ambience on offer.
Played both the Retropolis games & had such a good time, so was excited to see another game by Peanut Button, I was not disappointed, the horror vibe was great & the point & click is on keeping with their previous titles & works really well.
I do love a horror game in VR, I think I manage this one without closing my eyes , it’s a good one people & so cheap, don’t miss out on another exceptional puzzle game from the Peanuts.

developer_response.body: The Retropolis connection means a lot to us, and we're so glad the horror atmosphere and art style landed the way we hoped. Thanks for jumping in during early access and for recommending us to other players. Stay with us as we keep building out the manor.

reviews.review_description: Riddlewood Manor delivers a solid atmospheric puzzle-horror experience, There are a few moments where progression felt a bit unclear, that said i have really enjoyed playing it so far, and look forward to the next updates, definitely one for the library if you are a fan of escape room genres

developer_response.body: Really glad the atmosphere and puzzles are landing for you, and that note on progression clarity is something we're actively working to improve as we expand the manor. Stay with us as we build out more of Riddlewood, and if you enjoy puzzle adventures, our other Quest games are worth a look too.

reviews.review_description: its got some puzzles i have never seen before. so i have to think what i need to do to solve them which is not bad at all. I adore the art style and creepiness of the world. Movement style is a interesting choose i guess more for room scale.

developer_response.body: Those are exactly the kinds of puzzles we love designing, ones that make you stop and think without being unfair. Really glad the art style and atmosphere are landing for you too. Stay with us as we keep building out the manor in Early Access!

reviews.review_description:
This game has stellar voice acting, hand drawn artwork and a host of puzzling yet fair challenges. I believe this is better than the first Retropolis in it's current state and could continue to rival the second game if the developers keep up the amazing work.
Only complaint is a small bug when you die and respawn, when entering the house (at least in hand tracking) the inventory disappears. But other than that a five star point and click adventure!

developer_response.body: The Retropolis comparison genuinely means a lot to us, and we're thrilled the voice acting, artwork, and puzzles are all landing the way we hoped. The inventory disappearing on respawn when entering the house in hand tracking is a known bug we're actively working on. Thanks for playing early and sticking with us as we build this out.

reviews.review_description:
Just started playing this, I accept it’s early access, but it’s unforgivable to make you start from the beginning every time you restart the game..😱🤬
I had a disk save icon come upon entering the initial room, collected more things, etc 
Left the game, had to replay from the beginning, how much time do you think we all have.?
So, no matter how good the graphics are, or the cost, it’s just not acceptable to issue it as an early access in this state.
Given the honesty of the Devs, I’ve upgraded the Two Stars to Four, based on their working on a fix also..👏🏻👌

developer_response.body: The frustration here is completely fair, and we want to be transparent: we believe your progress was actually saved to your last save point rather than fully lost, but you spawned back at the start of the map rather than the room where you left off. That's on us, and we're actively working on fixing the respawn position so you return to the last room you reached. Thanks for sticking with us in early access, and we hope the next session feels a lot better.

reviews.review_description: Having played and enjoyed the charming Retropolis by the same developer, I was happy to try this in early access.  I am perhaps an hour into the game and so far the graphics are lovely and the puzzles are challenging and logical.  While the interface and the save system can use a bit more time cooking, I am really enjoying myself and looking forward to the release of the complete game.  If you enjoy a good puzzle adventure do not hesitate to jump on this early at the very attractive price.

developer_response.body: It's wonderful to hear that Retropolis brought you to Riddlewood Manor, and that the puzzles and visuals are doing their job. Your note on the interface and save system is well taken, and both are areas we're continuing to refine as development moves forward. Thanks for the kind recommendation and for being part of the early access journey with us.

reviews.review_title: A haunted house on memory lane
reviews.review_description:
I've been a fan of point and click adventures ever since playing Broken Sword back in the late 90s and I have been a fan of Peanut Button ever since playing Retropolis just a few years ago.
During the moments the game auto saves, you're greeted with the pop up of a floppy disk, which feels like a real nice little nod to the old school point and click games of yesteryear.
Retropolis was a taster and when the sequel released I had high expectations and it more than delivered on them, now Riddlewood Manor is their next creation and I am happy to report it keeps the same level of quality as their previous titles.
The visual style looks gorgeous, lots of black and red, the themes you'd want in a creepy old manor house that holds secrets in every room.
You'll want to play with headphones or earphones to benefit fully from the atmosphere the game is trying to create, although it has some tongue in cheek humour it definitely has unsettling and creepy occurrences going on and it finds a way to balance the two.
It's quite short, but it's in early access and only £3.99, it's a must buy at that price and I can't wait to see more of it.

developer_response.body: The floppy disk autosave icon is exactly the kind of detail we love sneaking in, so it's great that it landed for a fellow retro games veteran. Really glad Riddlewood Manor feels like a worthy follow-up to Retropolis, and your point about headphones is well taken as a tip for newcomers. Thanks for playing in early access, and there's plenty more of the manor still to come.

reviews.review_description: Riddlewood Manor is a super fun adventure, it reminds me a lot of the good old days of Point & Click Adventures. The atmosphere is cool 😎  with a spooky theme. The difficulty of the Puzzles is perfect imo, not too easy not to difficult, really on point.

developer_response.body: The point-and-click comparison genuinely made our day! That's exactly the spirit we set out to capture. So glad the puzzle difficulty is hitting the right balance for you. Thanks for playing early and stay with us as we continue to build out the manor!

reviews.review_title: Solve puzzles in a creepy environment
reviews.review_description:
This is my first ‘clicker’ game in VR. It was already on my wishlist. So happy that I finally could play this.

I love all kind of puzzle games and escape rooms. 

I can compare this game with a comic strip, with some creepy characters. 

For now only the 1st chapter is available, but other chapter gonna be added in the future (and no need to pay for the extra chapters).

Looking forward to the other chapters.

developer_response.body: The comic strip comparison is a great way to put it, and we're really glad Riddlewood Manor finally made it off your wishlist and into your hands. More chapters are on the way, so stay with us as we keep building the Manor out.

reviews.review_description:
Quite logical and not too difficult puzzles so far.
Handtracking works great which makes me happy since I have stick-drift on both controllers. I hope to see more games supporting it.
I like the artstyle and eagerly await next episode.

developer_response.body: Really glad hand tracking is working well for you, and that the puzzles and art style are landing the way we hoped. Thanks for playing during early access and stay with us as we build the next levels!

reviews.review_description: Awesome !!mor plzzzz more can’t wait! Now those r jump scares I will take all day!!!!Hurry and why aint u hit that purchase button yet! Better get it while u can for this price !!!

developer_response.body: Those jump scares are just the beginning! Thanks for playing during Early Access and for spreading the word. There's a lot more manor to explore as we keep building, stay with us on this ride.

reviews.review_description: There is a bug: the inventory is lost when i enter in a new area after i die

developer_response.body: Thanks for flagging this, and we're glad the game is catching your interest! The inventory loss on death when entering a new area is a known bug we're actively working on. Stay with us as we continue building out Riddlewood Manor.

reviews.review_description: I died and was reincarnated, but now I can't find my inventory. Is there a button or something to bring it up? Pretty simple game, but also kind of fun. Exited game and restarted and inventory came back. Died again and tried again and the option window was to my left and I couldn't turn to face it, but was able to continue, except, inventory was missing again. Restarted again and finished the demo and ready for more.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sticking with it and finishing the demo. Glad it was fun despite the frustration! What you're describing with the inventory disappearing after death, and the option window appearing out of view is a real bug that we want to nail down. We're looking into both issues now. We're actively building on the full game and want the experience to feel smooth from the start.

reviews.review_description: It's pretty much the same as the flat screen version, but in VR. It's still a locked in spot point and click style adventure, but the visual style in VR just makes it feel all the much more entertaining.

developer_response.body: Really glad the VR version is hitting the right notes for you. We're continuing to build on it as we expand the VR early access. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: Showing Lots of Promise So Far
reviews.review_description:
*UPDATE: It seems the conclusion I came to just going by the red circle drawings hint was correct, but my apparently tired mind wasn't perceiving the similarities that were obviously there and I was looking for at the time. So it seems the game's team have actually done just fine with the hints from what little I've played thus far. I can't wait to see what more the game offers now though!
It honestly makes me want to revisit the Retropolis games again as well.
Will definitely give an actual review when I complete it, but I do recommend it just from the promise it currently shows and the excellent art design and haunted house-vibes on top of being a VR puzzler (nothing better than that).
Just don't go in tired and get flustered by a very obvious puzzle at the very beginning as I did (read original "review"..😂) and I'm sure it'll be great for anyone who loves puzzle games. 


*OG:- I love how the game looks and it's general vibe, but just as soon as I get in the manor after just a minute of playing: I absolutely hit a wall. I get in the "Do Not Enter" room, and there is absolutely nothing I can think of to do with the place cards or what order to lay them in (the statues with the same symbols give no logical clue) and putting the 2 trains on the tracks did nothing. Outside of that obscure puzzle 2 minutes into the game? There's nothing to do. No clues, no other puzzles to solve, and no other items to pick up anywhere. 
So the puzzle is poorly thought out and it's the first real puzzle of the game...(bad start, that).

Most of my masdive VR library is puzzle games and escape room titles, so I hate running into another cool puzzler that doesn't understand how to naturally lead you in the right direction rather than putting you into a room with a few items, zero clues, and no logical reasoning behind figuring out the solution for yourself..
May have to get my $5 back sadly.
Seems to be a great piece of work outside of the puzzle design, but if I can't do anything in it then it's not worth my money.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing early and for taking the time to write this up so clearly. You're not the first to feel that the clues don't give enough to reason from, and that's genuinely useful to hear from someone with a deep escape room background. We'd love to dig into the specifics with you on our Discord, where we're working through exactly this kind of puzzle feedback as we build the game out.

reviews.review_description: There is something wonderful about simplicity. Having played the Retropolis games, I had to try this. I'm just started, getting past the first stage and it is a pleasure to play. If you like attractive puzzles and a comic book style environment, this is worth getting.

developer_response.body: Thank you for this, and for coming over from the Retropolis games to give Riddlewood Manor a try. We're glad the simplicity is landing the way we hoped. Stick with it as we keep building out the early access version.

reviews.review_description: Was really enjoying the game, way down into the lower area, I died, went back in and noticed my inventory had vanished entirely, pressing any button, recentering, nothing made it reapear.

developer_response.body: That inventory disappearing after death is a bug we're actively working on patching. In the meantime, a full game restart should bring it back. Thanks for playing early and sticking with us while we iron these things out.
Thanks so much for the kind words! If you ever want to share more thoughts or stay in the loop on what we're working on next, come find us in our community.

reviews.review_description: It is really fun and the hand drawn aesthetic and choppy Ami,action is gorgeous. Can't wait for the full release!

developer_response.body: Thank you! Hearing that the hand-drawn art and animation are landing the way we hoped genuinely means a lot to us. We're working hard on the full release and can't wait for you to see where the game goes from here. Hope you'll stick around for it!

reviews.review_description:
Update:

Reading other reviews I see that the vanishing inventory problem seems to be a glitch that's separate from the natural turning glitch.

Since there's been no response from the developers to the earlier reviews mentioning the vanishing inventory glitch I'm going to request a refund since I don't want to take a chance I'll get stuck with this game. I don't want to play it using artificial turning. It's frustrating owning a wireless headset that lets you freely turn naturally with your own feet when so many developers treat it as if it was tethered by a cable to a PC.

Hopefully it'll get proper standing support in the full release version.

Original Review:

I'm a fan of this studio's work but as much as I love the look of this game and the puzzle gameplay, I can't enjoy it because it only supports sedentary gameplay which makes it feel like playing a flat screen game on a TV.

If you play standing while turning with your feet the inventory system breaks. Sometimes you can manage to find the inventory panel and drag it around with you, but sometimes it just vanishes.

This game does not support standing play as defined in Meta's documentation. A game that supports standing play must not break if you turn with your feet.

It's maddening to be in a VR environment where I could be standing and free to turn and look around using my own feet yet be restricted to turning with a thumb stick.

I really want to experience this game, but I'm considering requesting a refund since it's too frustrating to play in its current state.

developer_response.body: Your frustration here is completely valid, and we especially hear you on the standing play issue. You're right that a game claiming standing support shouldn't break when you turn naturally with your feet, and the inventory disappearing in that context is a separate problem from the level-transition bug we're already tracking. We're actively working on both, and we're sorry we hadn't responded to earlier reviews mentioning it sooner. If you do request a refund, we understand, but we'd love to have you back when these are properly fixed as we continue building out Riddlewood Manor.

reviews.review_description: Part 1: An easy five star, this game has a lot of charm. From the beautifully drawn visuals, the cartoon artstyle and the intriguing puzzles. I personally loved going through this one and solving each of the puzzles, a personal favorite of mine being the cooking segment. I will not go into detail here for those who haven't gotten the game yet but know that it is now one of my favorites, and I cannot wait for the next part to release.

developer_response.body: The cooking segment has a special place in our hearts too 🐷 Really glad the puzzles, art style, and overall charm are hitting the mark. Stay with us as we continue building out Riddlewood Manor!

reviews.review_description: Absolutely love puzzle games and this keeps you interested without being overly complicated to figure out the puzzles. The fact it's point and click is great for people like me who get motion sickness. Can't wait to play the full version.

developer_response.body: So glad the puzzle balance is hitting the right spot, and great to hear the point-and-click format is working well for motion sensitivity. Thanks for playing Riddlewood Manor in early access and stay with us as we keep building it out.

reviews.review_description: Very clever puzzle game. Really enjoyed the first part. Only issue I had, which others have mentioned, I lost my inventory each time going into a new section. Got it back after I quit the game and re-entered.

developer_response.body: Really glad the puzzles are clicking for you, and thanks for flagging the inventory issue. We're aware of it and actively working on a fix. Stay with us as we build out the game!

reviews.review_description: Just finished the early release version. Great art style. Puzzles are creative and overall love the presentation. It left me hanging and I look forward to the full release.

developer_response.body: The art style and puzzles are exactly what we've been pouring ourselves into, so it's great to hear both are landing. Stay with us as we continue building out Riddlewood Manor!

reviews.review_description:
Turn on your brain box ,as this fun puzzle game gets

 you thinking. Died many times but I got addicted and had to jump back in. Looking forward for it to be continued...brilliant.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing Riddlewood Manor in early access and writing out, and stay with us as we keep building it out.
